title: Add AuthorityCertificate claim guard
rationale: Prevent terminal evidence from drifting into approval, payment, settlement, trust, certification, compliance, or success claims.
acceptance: Forbidden validity/approval/payment/trust terms fail claim checks in certificate/verifier-facing docs and user-facing strings.
Approved wording states AuthorityCertificate is terminal evidence only.
quality:claims passes with the new guard.
non_goals: Do not change certificate minting semantics.
Do not add hosted routes.

title: Introduce authority certificate issuer and key version read model
rationale: Verification needs protocol-owned issuer/key/role/algorithm/status/time-window records instead of ad hoc pinned key shape.
acceptance: Models exist for issuer, key version, signer role, algorithm, status, and validity window.
Existing local pinned verification consumes or adapts to the read model.
Unknown issuer, unknown key, role mismatch, retired key, and algorithm mismatch are distinct outcomes.
non_goals: Do not expose JWKS yet.
Do not fetch remote keys.

title: Create native verifier key-set projection
rationale: JWKS must be a projection from a native trust model, not the authority source.
acceptance: Native verifier key-set projection exposes only allowed public metadata and key material.
Projection includes issuer, key id/version, role, algorithm, status, and time-window fields where appropriate.
Private key material is never projected.
non_goals: Do not claim key discovery establishes trust.
Do not implement hosted metadata yet.

title: Add JWKS projection from verifier key set
rationale: External verifiers need RFC 7517-compatible public key representation without inheriting OAuth or CA semantics.
acceptance: JWKS emits public key material only.
Duplicate key ids, malformed JWK material, and algorithm confusion have negative tests.
Docs or protocol comments state JWKS is projection, not trust.
non_goals: Do not publish private key material.
Do not accept certificate-supplied jwks_uri as authority.

title: Add authority certificate status records
rationale: Verification must fail closed on revoked, stale, unknown, and status-unavailable states.
acceptance: Status record model represents active, retired, revoked, stale, unknown, and unavailable outcomes.
Revoked and stale cannot return verified.
Status unavailable returns proof_gap with explicit reason.
non_goals: Do not borrow OAuth token revocation semantics wholesale.
Do not make status lookup mutating.

title: Define structured verification request and response
rationale: Consumers need audit-safe categories, not a boolean validity shortcut.
acceptance: Request and response models exist for authority certificate verification.
Response separates schema, crypto, signer trust, role, key status, artifact match, gateway binding, terminal binding, status, and failure reasons.
No boolean-only isValid-style API is introduced.
non_goals: Do not expose raw receipts or secrets.
Do not imply authorization or payment success.

title: Add hosted verifier metadata, key-set, JWKS, and status routes
rationale: External auditors and receivers need non-mutating discovery and status surfaces before hosted verification.
acceptance: Routes expose metadata, native key-set projection, JWKS projection, and status lookup.
Routes are non-mutating and redacted.
Metadata may include jwks_uri but does not inherit OAuth semantics or establish trust.
non_goals: Do not add verify route side effects.
Do not fetch remote trust material.

title: Add hosted certificate verification route
rationale: Hosted verifier must answer the external evidence question without becoming a mutation or leakage channel.
acceptance: Route deterministically parses bounded requests.
Oversized, malformed, unknown issuer, revoked, stale, and status-unavailable cases fail closed.
Response is redacted and excludes raw receipts, gateway credentials, secrets, and principal-private context.
non_goals: Do not mutate certificate, receipt, key, issuer, or status state.
Do not return authorized, approved, paid, settled, trusted, certified, or compliant.

title: Update CLI verification output for structured parity
rationale: CLI, SDK, and MCP verification surfaces must preserve evidence-plane semantics consistently.
acceptance: CLI cert verify reports structured categories matching protocol response.
CLI remains non-mutating.
CLI does not expose boolean-only validity language.
non_goals: Do not add mutation commands.
Do not make CLI output settlement or payment proof.

title: Define x402 exact per-call evidence profile
rationale: x402 is the first protected-action wedge and needs a narrow evidence profile after verifier primitives exist.
acceptance: Profile states what AuthorityCertificate proves and fails to prove for one exact paid HTTP call.
Profile does not claim settlement, payment success, delivery, authorization, or compliance.
External receiver/auditor flow can verify certificate against published keys/current status and inspect exact protected-action evidence.
non_goals: Do not create a payment clearing house.
Do not generalize beyond exact per-call protected-action evidence.

title: Run closeout quality and architecture gates
rationale: The trust plane is only credible if claim, architecture, formatting, focused tests, and repo checks all pass.
acceptance: quality:claims passes.
quality:architecture passes.
format:check passes.
check:repo passes.
Focused authority-certificate, verifier, certificate, http, and cli tests pass or substitutions are recorded.
non_goals: Do not implement additional product scope during closeout.
Do not clean unrelated dirty worktree drift.
